title: Salty Norwegian Peanut butter Flatbread
description: A crispy and savory flatbread made with long-fermentation-dough, peanut butter, and a hint of salt, perfect for a sick day.
instructions: Combine flour, warm water, yeast, and salt in a large mixing bowl
Mix the dough until it forms a sticky ball, then knead for 5 minutes
Place the dough in a greased bowl, cover it with plastic wrap, and let it rise in a warm place for 2 hours
Roll out the dough to a thickness of 1/4 inch, then transfer it to a baking sheet
Spread peanut butter evenly over the dough, leaving a 1/2 inch border around the edges
Sprinkle sesame seeds and sugar over the peanut butter
Drizzle olive oil over the top of the flatbread
Bake the flatbread in the preheated oven for 15-20 minutes, or until it's golden brown and crispy
Remove the flatbread from the oven and let it cool on a wire rack for 5 minutes
Cut the flatbread into 8-10 pieces and serve immediately
